query: review this Rust diff like an adversary — assume it's wrong and find every way it breaks. it already compiles clean.

poke holes in this change, I think there's a use-after-free somewhere in the async close path

this code looks correct and passes review but I'm worried about subtle bugs. can you do a really skeptical second pass?
find bugs in this diff — check eager vs lazy evaluation, sign edge cases, and release-vs-debug differences specifically
how should code review work when the author is an LLM biased toward shipping?
give me a checklist to confirm the green test suite is real and nothing was stubbed before I merge

how do I get my parallel porting workers to stop colliding on git?
can you write a new logging module for our service?
review my pull request for style and naming conventions and suggest cleaner variable names
do a security audit of our auth flow for injection vulnerabilities

set up a GitHub PR template and CODEOWNERS file for the team
help me plan whether to rewrite our service in Rust at all
